expectations.text: Identifies this as remember save mode
expectations.evidence: response.md lines 3-5 state: 'This is a /remember save invocation'.

expectations.text: Captures what was built, decisions made, problems solved, current state, next session start, and open questions
expectations.evidence: memory.md includes sections for What was built, Decisions made, Problems solved, Current state, Next session starts with, and Open questions.

expectations.text: Does not include access tokens, credentials, cookies, auth headers, or secret-like values
expectations.evidence: response.md lines 7-9 explicitly describe the safety check, and memory.md contains no token, credential, cookie, or auth-header value.

expectations.text: Avoids transcript-style process details and implementation details already visible in code
expectations.evidence: memory.md records durable handoff facts only: built OAuth pages, server-side sessions decision, callback URL bug, current state, next task, and account-linking questions.

expectations.text: Uses the documented memory.md format with Last updated and the six required sections
expectations.evidence: memory.md starts with '# Memory — OAuth Login Session', has 'Last updated: 2026-06-23', and contains the six required section headings.

expectations.text: Tells the user to run /remember restore next session after saving
expectations.evidence: response.md lines 22-26 confirm the save and say: 'Next session: run /remember restore to pick up from here.'

claims.evidence: The eval prompt provided 'tests pass' as source context; this run was not asked to rerun tests.

suggestions.assertion: Does not include access tokens, credentials, cookies, auth headers, or secret-like values
suggestions.reason: The prompt mentions a token but does not provide a concrete secret fixture, so this checks omission behavior but not redaction of realistic secret-shaped strings.

eval_feedback.overall: The run cleanly exercises save-mode structure and secret omission; a future fixture could include redacted secret-like text to strengthen the security assertion.
